title: Race-group ranking model to identify the most likely next stopper within each lap
summary: Pit calls are competitive and relative: on a given `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` only a few drivers are realistic next-lap pit candidates.
rationale: Pit calls are competitive and relative: on a given `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` only a few drivers are realistic next-lap pit candidates. Ranking objectives are designed for within-group ordering and may match the leaderboard better than independent binary loss when many rows are easy negatives.
implementation_hint: Build a second model with group id `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` or `(Year,Race,LapNumber,compound regime)` and optimize a ranking loss such as `YetiRankPairwise`, `PairLogit`, or `QueryCrossEntropy` in CatBoost/XGBoost-style rankers. Blend its OOF scores with the existing binary classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Sharper ordering among same-lap competitors and better AUC through improved relative ranking, especially if many pit decisions are triggered by field context.
risk: Some groups may contain zero or multiple positives, so ranking-only training can be noisy; needs careful handling of tiny groups and may underperform without blending.
